About: It is about austerity-era Britain, culture, and working class life.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the mod subculture.
It is associated with musical sources like the Wu-Tang Clan.
It is associated with Spiritualized.
It is associated with Bent.
It is associated with Roni Size.
It is associated with The Prodigy.
It is associated with Leftfield.
It is associated with IMPALA.
It is associated with Harbinger Sound.
It is associated with Rough Trade Records.
ConsistsOf: It consists of vocalist Jason Williamson and instrumentalist Andrew Fearn.
CreatedBy: It was created by Jason Williamson and Simon Parfrement.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by abrasive, minimalist musical style.
It is characterized by embittered explorations of austerity-era Britain.
It is characterized by embittered explorations of culture.
It is characterized by embittered explorations of working class life.
It is characterized by Williamson's East Midlands accent.
It is characterized by electronic munt minimalist punk-hop rants for the working class.
It is characterized by embittered rants about unemployment.
It is characterized by embittered rants about modern working life.
It is characterized by embittered rants about celebrities and pop culture.
It is characterized by embittered rants about capitalism and society in general.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the mod subculture.
It was influenced by musical sources like the Wu-Tang Clan.
It was influenced by the Wu-Tang Clan.
It was influenced by Stone Roses.
It was influenced by Red Snapper.
It was influenced by Two Lone Swordsmen.
IsA: It is an English post-punk music duo.
KnownFor: It is known for their abrasive, minimalist musical style.
It is known for embittered explorations of austerity-era Britain.
It is known for embittered explorations of culture.
It is known for embittered explorations of working class life.
It is known for Williamson's East Midlands accent.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Burton upon Trent.
PartOf: It is part of the music industry.
ProducedBy: It was produced by a studio engineer at Rubber Biscuit Studio.
It was produced by Andrew Fearn.
PublishedAs: It was published as the lyrics collection Grammar Wanker: Sleaford Mods 2007-2014.
It was published as Jason Williamson's House Party: Sleaford Mods 2014-2019.
It was published as the T.C.R. EP.
It was published as English Tapas.
It was published as Eton Alive.
It was published as All That Glue.
It was published as Spare Ribs.
It was published as UK Grim.
It was published as The Demise of Planet X.
It was published as "The Good Life".
PublishedBy: It was published by Rough Trade Records.
It was published by Extreme Eating Records.
PublishedIn: It was published in March 2015.
It was published in August 2015.
It was published in July 2015.
It was published in 2016.
It was published in 2017.
It was published in 2019.
It was published in 2020.
It was published in 2021.
It was published on 30 October 2020.
It was published on 20 October 2022.
UsedIn: It is used in the series finale of Peaky Blinders.
WrittenBy: It was written by Jason Williamson.
WrittenIn: It was written in East Midlands.
